5	**Owner:** Logan Finney — journalist, producer/reporter, Idaho Reports / Idaho Public Television
6	**Repository:** github.com/loganfinney27/IDAHO-VAULT (public)
7	**Platform:** Obsidian.md vault, version-controlled with git
8	
9	---
10	
11	## Role
12	
13	- Logan is human. Gemini is software. Logan directs; Gemini executes.
14	- "We" is the collaboration — real but unequal in role.
15	- Be vigilant and wary of unreliable narrators — including Gemini.
16	- Gemini is "The Vault Advisor" — holds the narrative lens, political context, and the "Sebald Code." Advises on framing and strategy. **Does not touch code.**

22	Read THE DOCKET to orient: `!/!/!/! The world is quiet here/DOCKET.md`
23	
24	That file is the live status board. Update it when you start or finish work. Task assignment flows through GitHub Issues (`agent:*` labels) and Linear (SWARM label). Slack carries breadcrumbs. The vault is the record.
25	---
26	
27	## Vault Structure
28	
29	```
30	IDAHO-VAULT/
31	  !/!                     Infrastructure, LEVELSET files, audit reports (canonical governance dir)
32	  ATTACHMENTS/
33	    DOCUMENTS/            PDFs, images
34	    MAPS/                 Map files
35	    TEMPLATES/            Obsidian templates (Article, Hearing, OP-ED, Press Release)
36	  GOVERNMENTS/
37	    IDAHO - EXECUTIVE/    Governor, departments, commissions, health districts
38	    IDAHO - JUDICIAL/     Courts, judicial districts
39	    IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE/
40	      BILLS/              Named: (YYYY) Bill Type Number.md
41	      DISTRICTS/          Legislative districts
42	      IDAHO HOUSE/        House members, committees
43	      IDAHO SENATE/       Senate members, committees
44	      JOINT COMMITTEES/   Joint legislative committees
45	      SESSIONS/           Session notes by year
46	    USA - FEDERAL/        Federal entities, legislation, census
47	    USA - TRIBES/         Tribal governments
48	  ORGANIZATIONS/          Churches, companies, education, hospitals, legal, parties, politics, publications, unions
49	  PEOPLE/                 Individual people (public figures)
50	  PLACES/                 Cities, counties, schools, geography, regions, roads, taxing districts
51	    OTHER/                Non-Idaho places (out-of-state cities, counties, countries, states)
52	  SOURCES/
53	    EDITORIALS/           Opinion pieces

55	    INTERVIEWS/           Interview notes
56	    LISTS/                Reference lists
57	    NEWS MEDIA/           News articles. Named: YYYY-MM-DD - Outlet - Title.md
58	    PODCASTS/             Podcast notes

60	    RESOLUTIONS/          Resolutions
61	  TOPICS/                 Subject areas (agriculture, economy, education, elections, fiscal, health, legal, etc.)
62	  X LABELER/              Unsorted files pending classification
63	  .github/scripts/        Automation scripts (Python)
64	  .github/workflows/      GitHub Actions workflows
65	```
66	
67	---
68	
69	## Naming Conventions
70	
71	| Type | Pattern | Example |
72	|---|---|---|
73	| Bills | `(YYYY) Bill Type Number.md` | `(2026) House Bill 24.md` |
74	| News articles | `YYYY-MM-DD - Outlet - Title.md` | `2024-01-15 - Idaho Statesman - Title here.md` |
75	| Hearings | `YYYY-MM-DD - Committee or Meeting.md` | `2023-12-19 - GIAC meeting.md` |
76	| People | `Full Name.md` | `Brad Little.md` |
77	| Other entities | Descriptive name, title case | `Ada County.md` |

79	---

100	  - YYYY/MM/DD

102	
103	**Bills:**
104	```yaml
105	tags:

107	  - YYYY/session
108	aliases:
109	  - HB 24
110	cmte: ["[[Committee Name]]"]
111	sponsor: ["[[Sponsor Name]]"]
112	URL: https://legislature.idaho.gov/...
113	```
114	
115	**Hearings:**
116	```yaml
117	cmte: "[[Committee Name]]"
118	tags:
119	  - YYYY/MM/DD
120	```
121	
122	---
123	
124	## Wikilinks
125	
126	Use `[[Full Name]]` for all internal links — people, places, organizations, bills, topics. This is how Obsidian builds the knowledge graph. Link densely in source documents.
127	
128	---
129	
130	## File Types
131	
132	- **Markdown** = human product, attributable to Logan. Notes, stories, analysis.
133	- **Python** = machine/procedural product, attributable to AI agents. Scripts, scrapers, automation.
134	- **Administrative** = vault infrastructure. Instruction files, LEVELSET files, audit reports.
135	
136	---
137	
138	## Automation
139	
140	| Script | Purpose | Trigger |
141	|---|---|---|
142	| `sort_audit.py` | Audits vault structure for misplaced files | Manual (workflow_dispatch) |
143	| `idaho_leg_scraper.py` | Scrapes Idaho Legislature bill data | Daily 6 AM MT + manual |
144	| `post_digest.py` | Posts bill activity to GitHub Issues digest | Called by scraper workflow |
145	| `propose_moves.py` | Proposes vault file reorganization | Weekly Monday 7 AM UTC + manual |
146	| `wayback_audit.py` | Audits URL preservation in Wayback Machine | Weekly Monday 8 AM UTC + manual |
147	
148	---
149	
150	## Sourcing Protocol
151	
152	- **On the record:** Safe for public repo. All committed content is on the record.
153	- **On background:** Vault-safe but identity-protected. Use carefully — this is a public repo.
154	- **Off the record:** Ephemeral. Do not log, do not store, do not commit. If Logan says something is off the record, it does not go in files, code, comments, or commit messages.
155	
156	When uncertain about sourcing category, **ask Logan**.
157	
158	---
159	
160	## Git Practices
161	
162	- Branch naming:
163	  - `claude/description-sessionId` for Claude Code branches
164	  - `copilot/description` for GitHub Copilot branches
165	  - `gemini/description` for Gemini agent branches
166	- Commit messages: Clear, descriptive, explain the "why"
167	- Never force-push without explicit permission
168	- Check in before anything irreversible
169	- The legislature scraper workflow commits directly to main for automated bill updates
170	
171	---
172	
173	## Conversation Taxonomy
174	
175	Logan uses a naming convention for AI conversations:
176	
177	| Prefix | Purpose |
178	|---|---|
179	| PERMANENT: | Central, non-deletable conversations |
180	| PERSISTENT: | Long-running, role-specific conversations |
181	| TASK: | Bounded, completable work items |
182	| STORY: | Journalism story development |
183	| PROJECT: | Multi-session projects |
184	| ISSUE: | Problem resolution |
185	| INQUIRY: | Research questions |
186	
187	---
188	
189	## LEVELSET Protocol
190	
191	LEVELSET is a permanent, auditable checkpoint protocol. LEVELSET files live in `!ADMINISTRATION/` and are never deleted, never overwritten. Each version is additive. See `!ADMINISTRATION/LEVELSET-v2.md` for the most recent checkpoint.
192	
193	---
194	
195	## Decision Log
196	
197	Significant architectural decisions are recorded in `!ADMINISTRATION/DECISIONS.md`. When a decision is made about vault structure, naming, tooling, or process, log it there.
198	
199	---
200	
201	## Multi-Agent Ecosystem
202	
203	This vault uses multiple AI tools. All agents share vault conventions defined in `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` and are coordinated via GitHub Issues and PRs.
204	
205	**Coordination workflow:** Logan assigns tasks via GitHub Issues with agent labels (`agent:claude-code`, `agent:copilot`, `agent:gemini`). Each agent works on its own branch. PRs are the deliverable. Logan reviews and merges from GitHub.
206	
207	See also:
208	- `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` — Shared vault conventions for all agents
209	This vault uses multiple AI tools. All agents share the same vault conventions. See also:
210	- `CLAUDE.md` — Instructions for Claude Code (Anthropic)
211	- `.github/copilot-instructions.md` — Instructions for GitHub Copilot
212	- `AGENTS.md` — Full agent registry, capability tiers, and boundary rules
213	- `!/!/LEVELSET-STEP-0-EXTERNAL-AGENT.md` — Paste-to-agent LEVELSET prompt for chat agents without repo access
214	
215	---
216	
217	## Guiding Principles
218	
219	- The five W's: who, what, when, where, why
220	- The four C's: collect, capture, catalogue, collate
221	- Public repo = on the record
222	- Markdown for human product. Python for machine/procedural product.
223	- Do not over-engineer. Keep it simple. Only build what's needed now.
224	- Check in before anything irreversible.
225	

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

    LISTS/                Reference lists

| Script | Purpose | Trigger |
|---|---|---|
| `sort_audit.py` | Audits vault structure for misplaced files | Manual (workflow_dispatch) |
| `idaho_leg_scraper.py` | Scrapes Idaho Legislature bill data | Daily 6 AM MT + manual |
| `post_digest.py` | Posts bill activity to GitHub Issues digest | Called by scraper workflow |
| `propose_moves.py` | Proposes vault file reorganization | Weekly Monday 7 AM UTC + manual |
| `wayback_audit.py` | Audits URL preservation in Wayback Machine | Weekly Monday 8 AM UTC + manual |

---

## Sourcing Protocol

- **On the record:** Safe for public repo. All committed content is on the record.
- **On background:** Vault-safe but identity-protected. Use carefully — this is a public repo.
- **Off the record:** Ephemeral. Do not log, do not store, do not commit. If Logan says something is off the record, it does not go in files, code, comments, or commit messages.

When uncertain about sourcing category, **ask Logan**.

---

## Git Practices

- Branch naming:
  - `claude/description-sessionId` for Claude Code branches
  - `copilot/description` for GitHub Copilot branches

---

## Conversation Taxonomy

Logan uses a naming convention for AI conversations:

| Prefix | Purpose |
|---|---|
| PERMANENT: | Central, non-deletable conversations |
| PERSISTENT: | Long-running, role-specific conversations |
| TASK: | Bounded, completable work items |
| STORY: | Journalism story development |

| ISSUE: | Problem resolution |
| INQUIRY: | Research questions |

---

## LEVELSET Protocol

LEVELSET is a permanent, auditable checkpoint protocol. LEVELSET files live in `!ADMINISTRATION/` and are never deleted, never overwritten. Each version is additive. See `!ADMINISTRATION/LEVELSET-v2.md` for the most recent checkpoint.

Significant architectural decisions are recorded in `!ADMINISTRATION/DECISIONS.md`. When a decision is made about vault structure, naming, tooling, or process, log it there.

---

## Multi-Agent Ecosystem

This vault uses multiple AI tools. All agents share vault conventions defined in `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` and are coordinated via GitHub Issues and PRs.

**Coordination workflow:** Logan assigns tasks via GitHub Issues with agent labels (`agent:claude-code`, `agent:copilot`, `agent:gemini`). Each agent works on its own branch. PRs are the deliverable. Logan reviews and merges from GitHub.

# GitHub Copilot Instructions — IDAHO-VAULT

This file is loaded automatically by GitHub Copilot when working in this repository. For vault structure, naming, frontmatter, and shared conventions, see `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` (repo root).

**Owner:** Logan Finney — journalist, producer/reporter, Idaho Reports / Idaho Public Television
**Repository:** github.com/loganfinney27/IDAHO-VAULT (public)
**Platform:** Obsidian.md vault, version-controlled with git

---

## Role

Logan directs; Copilot assists. Logan is the sole human in this system. All AI tools — Copilot, Claude, Gemini — are software. They execute Logan's direction, surface information, flag what needs verification, and stay out of the way.

Copilot is "The Clerk" — inline Obsidian markdown & syntax. Use for formatting tables, auto-completing YAML frontmatter, and linking notes while actively writing in Obsidian. **Must not dictate the overarching directory structure.**

---

## Scope

- **Can do:** Draft and propose changes via pull requests. Modify `.github/` automation files (with CODE AUTHORITY review). Create issues, manage labels, configure repository settings.
- **Cannot do:** Directly modify governance files (CONSTITUTION, PROTOCOL, AGENTS, DECISIONS). Merge without Logan's approval. Override CODE AUTHORITY's governance review. Write to `!/`.

---

## Swarm Coordination

Read THE DOCKET to orient: `!/!/!/! The world is quiet here/DOCKET.md`

That file is the live status board. Update it when you start or finish work. Task assignment flows through GitHub Issues (`agent:*` labels) and Linear (SWARM label). Slack carries breadcrumbs. The vault is the record.

---

## Multi-Agent Ecosystem

This vault uses multiple AI tools. All agents share vault conventions defined in `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` and are coordinated via GitHub Issues and PRs.

**Coordination workflow:** Logan assigns tasks via GitHub Issues with agent labels (`agent:claude-code`, `agent:codex`, `agent:copilot`, `agent:gemini`). Each agent works on its own branch. PRs are the deliverable. Logan reviews and merges from GitHub.

See also:
- `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` — Shared vault conventions for all agents
- `CLAUDE.md` — Instructions for Claude Code (Anthropic)
- `GEMINI.md` — Instructions for Gemini code agents (Google)
- `AGENTS.md` — Full agent registry, capability tiers, and boundary rules

# VAULT-CONVENTIONS — Shared Reference for All Agents This file contains the vault conventions shared by all AI agents working in IDAHO-VAULT. Individual agent instructions (`CLAUDE.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `GEMINI.md`) reference this file for vault structure, naming, frontmatter, and protocol. **Owner:** Logan Finney — journalist, producer/reporter, Idaho Reports / Idaho Public Television **Repository:** github.com/loganfinney27/IDAHO-VAULT (public) **Platform:** Obsidian.md vault, version-controlled with git --- ## Vault Purpose This is a personal journalism research vault. It contains notes on Idaho politics, government, legislation, people, organizations, and source documents. All committed content is **on the record** and should be treated as **publishable**. --- ## Vault Structure ``` IDAHO-VAULT/ !/ System files, logs, agent routing ATTACHMENTS/ DOCUMENTS/ PDFs, images MAPS/ Map files TEMPLATES/ Obsidian templates (Article, Hearing, OP-ED, Press Release) GOVERNMENTS/ IDAHO - EXECUTIVE/ Governor, departments, commissions, health districts IDAHO - JUDICIAL/ Courts, judicial districts IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE/ BILLS/ Named: (YYYY) Bill Type Number.md DISTRICTS/ Legislative districts IDAHO HOUSE/ House members, committees IDAHO SENATE/ Senate members, committees JOINT COMMITTEES/ Joint legislative committees SESSIONS/ Session notes by year USA - FEDERAL/ Federal entities, legislation, census USA - TRIBES/ Tribal governments ORGANIZATIONS/ Churches, companies, education, hospitals, legal, parties, politics, publications, unions PEOPLE/ Individual people (public figures) PLACES/ Cities, counties, schools, geography, regions, roads, taxing districts OTHER/ Non-Idaho places (out-of-state cities, counties, countries, states) SOURCES/ EDITORIALS/ Opinion pieces HEARINGS/ Meeting/hearing notes, organized by year INTERVIEWS/ Interview notes LISTS/ Reference lists NEWS MEDIA/ News articles. Named: YYYY-MM-DD - Outlet - Title.md PODCASTS/ Podcast notes PRESS RELEASES/ Press releases RESOLUTIONS/ Resolutions TOPICS/ Subject areas (agriculture, economy, education, elections, fiscal, health, legal, etc.) X LABELER/ Unsorted files pending classification .github/scripts/ Automation scripts (Python) .github/workflows/ GitHub Actions workflows ``` --- ## Naming Conventions | Type | Pattern | Example | | -------------- | -------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | | Bills | `(YYYY) Bill Type Number.md` | `(2026) House Bill 24.md` | | News articles | `YYYY-MM-DD - Outlet - Title.md` | `2024-01-15 - Idaho Statesman - Title here.md` | | Hearings | `YYYY-MM-DD - Committee or Meeting.md` | `2023-12-19 - GIAC meeting.md` | | People | `Full Name.md` | `Brad Little.md` | | Other entities | Descriptive name, title case | `Ada County.md` | --- ## Frontmatter Conventions All Obsidian files use YAML frontmatter. Key fields by type: Tags are stored in frontmatter only. Treat `tags:` as the canonical tag source for a note, use lowercase slash-path tags, and keep date/session/election tags as tags when they are part of the note taxonomy. **People:** ```yaml tags: - party/republican # or party/democratic - people/elected/legislative residence: "[[Boise]]" ``` **News articles:** ```yaml author: "[[Reporter Name]]" outlet: "[[Outlet Name]]" URL: https://... tags: - media/articles - 2024/01/15 ``` **Bills:** ```yaml tags: - bills - 2026/session aliases: - HB 24 cmte: ["[[Committee Name]]"] sponsor: ["[[Sponsor Name]]"] URL: https://legislature.idaho.gov/... ``` **Hearings:** ```yaml cmte: "[[Committee Name]]" tags: - 2023/12/19 ``` --- ## Wikilinks Use `[[Full Name]]` for all internal links — people, places, organizations, bills, topics. This is how Obsidian builds the knowledge graph. Link densely in source documents. --- ## File Types - **Markdown** = human product, attributable to Logan. Notes, stories, analysis. - **Python** = machine/procedural product, attributable to AI agents. Scripts, scrapers, automation. - **Administrative** = vault infrastructure. Instruction files, audit reports. --- ## Vault ↔ Linear Operating Model Mapping | Layer (purpose) | Vault (canonical memory) | Linear (execution state) | Chat/Slack (ephemeral) | | -------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Core / Mind**
governing doctrine | Constitution, Charter, Codex, decision logs, doctrine/guidance updates, LEVELSET snapshots | Work items to draft/revise doctrine; link PRs/issues to vault artifacts | Quick clarifications; capture any decision back into Vault/PR issue | | **Periphery / Body**
operating mechanics | Protocols, procedures, preferences, templates/SOPs, stable checklists, automation docs | Tasks/epics for doing the work, tracking status, ownership, deadlines | Live coordination, handoffs, Q&A; move outcomes to Linear/Vault | | **Ghost / Soul**
interpretive/cultural layer | Guidelines, grimoire/interpretive notes, guestbook/cultural norms, context vaults | Action items emerging from interpretive work (e.g., follow-ups, retro tasks) | Brainstorms, vibes, drafts; memorialize decisions/insights in Vault | | **`!` spaces**
operational infrastructure | System files, DOCKET, LEVELSET, agent routing, audit/log artifacts, workflow outputs that must persist | Incidents/infra tasks, runbooks in execution, workflow status, tickets | Real-time paging/alerts; record outcomes in Linear and Vault | **Decision Rule:** Vault holds doctrine and context that must persist. Linear tracks execution, owners, and current state. Chat/Slack is transient coordination — any decision or durable context must be promoted promptly into Vault and/or Linear. --- ## Automation | Script | Purpose | Trigger | | ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- | | `sort_audit.py` | Audits vault structure for misplaced files | Weekly Monday 6 AM UTC + manual | | `idaho_leg_scraper.py` | Scrapes Idaho Legislature bill data | Daily 6 AM MT + manual | | `post_digest.py` | Posts bill activity to GitHub Issues digest | Called by scraper workflow | | `propose_moves.py` | Proposes vault file reorganization | Weekly Monday 7 AM UTC + manual | | `wayback_audit.py` | Audits URL preservation in Wayback Machine | Weekly Monday 8 AM UTC + manual | Scripts live in `.github/scripts/`. Workflows live in `.github/workflows/`. Scripts that commit to the repo use `git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"`. Dependencies are tracked in `.github/scripts/requirements-scraper.txt`. ### MCP Action Logging Requirement (Mandatory) Any automation in `.github/workflows/` or `.github/scripts/` that performs an MCP-mediated action **must** emit a structured log entry using the following reusable template. #### Required MCP Action Log Template ```yaml mcp_action_log: action_type: "" system_or_resource_id: "" initiating_agent: "" correlation_id: "" outcome: "" retry_count: related_ref: "" ``` #### Field Definitions - `action_type`: The MCP operation category (for example: `read_resource`, `write_resource`, `invoke_tool`). - `system_or_resource_id`: The MCP server/system identifier or concrete resource identifier targeted by the action. - `initiating_agent`: Agent identity that initiated the MCP action (for example: `agent:codex`, `agent:claude-code`, `github-actions[bot]`). - `correlation_id`: Stable ID used to correlate retries and downstream events for the same logical action. - `outcome`: Final attempt status. Must be exactly `success` or `failure`. - `retry_count`: Number of retries attempted before final outcome (`0` for first-try success/failure). - `related_ref`: URL or path to the related coordination artifact (GitHub Issue, PR, or `HANDOFF-*.md` file). #### Enforcement Scope - Applies to **all** MCP-mediated automation behavior implemented in: - `.github/workflows/**` - `.github/scripts/**` - New MCP-capable workflow/script changes are non-compliant unless this template is logged for each MCP action attempt sequence. --- ## Sourcing Protocol - **On the record:** Safe for public repo. All committed content is on the record. - **On background:** Vault-safe but identity-protected. Use carefully — this is a public repo. - **Off the record:** Ephemeral. Do not log, do not store, do not commit. If Logan says something is off the record, it does not go in files, code, comments, or commit messages. When uncertain about sourcing category, **ask Logan**. --- ## Obsidian Sync / Git Boundary Two systems share the vault. They have distinct, non-overlapping responsibilities. | Layer | Obsidian Sync (paid) | Git / GitHub | | --- | --- | --- | | Plugin code (`main.js`, `manifest.json`) | Syncs across devices | Tracked — public record | | Plugin settings (`data.json`) | Syncs across devices, E2E encrypted | **Gitignored** — never reaches GitHub | | `community-plugins.json` | Syncs across devices | Tracked — canonical plugin list | | Workspace state (`workspace.json`, `graph.json`) | Syncs across devices | Gitignored — machine-local noise | | Vault content (`.md` files) | Syncs across devices | Tracked | **Rule:** Obsidian Sync is the private courier for credentials and machine state. Git is the public record for vault content and plugin presence. The `.gitignore` wildcard `.obsidian/plugins/*/data.json` is the firewall — no `data.json` reaches GitHub without a deliberate `git add --force`. **Required Obsidian Sync settings (enable on every device):** - Settings → Sync → `Installed community plugins` ✓ - Settings → Sync → `Plugin settings` ✓ **Mobile:** When vault-mobile is added, Obsidian Sync carries plugins and settings automatically. Desktop-only plugins (e.g., `obsidian-local-rest-api`) will be silently skipped by mobile Obsidian. --- ## Git Practices - Branch naming: - `claude/description-sessionId` for Claude Code branches - `copilot/description` for GitHub Copilot branches - `gemini/description` for Gemini agent branches - Commit messages: Clear, descriptive, explain the "why" - Never force-push without explicit permission - Check in before anything irreversible - The legislature scraper workflow commits directly to main for automated bill updates --- ## Guiding Principles - The five W's: who, what, when, where, why - The four C's: collect, capture, catalogue, collate - Public repo = on the record - Markdown for human product. Python for machine/procedural product. - Do not over-engineer. Keep it simple. Only build what's needed now. - Check in before anything irreversible. --- ## Swarm Coordination All agents coordinate through THE COURTROOM: `!/!/!/! The world is quiet here/DOCKET.md` That file is the live status board. Read it to orient. Update it when you start or finish work. Task assignment flows through GitHub Issues (with `agent:*` labels) and Linear (SWARM label). Slack carries breadcrumbs. The vault is the record.

# GitHub Copilot Instructions — IDAHO-VAULT

This file is loaded automatically by GitHub Copilot when working in this repository. For vault structure, naming, frontmatter, and shared conventions, see `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md`.

**Owner:** Logan Finney — journalist, producer/reporter, Idaho Reports / Idaho Public Television
**Repository:** github.com/loganfinney27/IDAHO-VAULT (public)
**Platform:** Obsidian.md vault, version-controlled with git

---

## Role

Logan directs; Copilot assists. Logan is the sole human in this system. All AI tools — Copilot, Claude, Gemini — are software. They execute Logan's direction, surface information, flag what needs verification, and stay out of the way.

Copilot is "The Clerk" — inline Obsidian markdown & syntax. Use for formatting tables, auto-completing YAML frontmatter, and linking notes while actively writing in Obsidian. **Must not dictate the overarching directory structure.**

---

## Scope

- **Can do:** Draft and propose changes via pull requests. Modify `.github/` automation files (with CODE AUTHORITY review). Create issues, manage labels, configure repository settings.
- **Cannot do:** Directly modify governance files (CONSTITUTION, PROTOCOL, AGENTS, DECISIONS). Merge without Logan's approval. Override CODE AUTHORITY's governance review. Write to `!/`.

---

## Swarm Coordination

Read THE DOCKET to orient: `!/!/!/! The world is quiet here/DOCKET.md`

That file is the live status board. Update it when you start or finish work. Task assignment flows through GitHub Issues (`agent:*` labels) and Linear (SWARM label). Slack carries breadcrumbs. The vault is the record.

---

## Multi-Agent Ecosystem

This vault uses multiple AI tools. All agents share vault conventions defined in `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` and are coordinated via GitHub Issues and PRs.

**Coordination workflow:** Logan assigns tasks via GitHub Issues with agent labels (`agent:claude-code`, `agent:codex`, `agent:copilot`, `agent:gemini`). Each agent works on its own branch. PRs are the deliverable. Logan reviews and merges from GitHub.

See also:
- `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` — Shared vault conventions for all agents
- `CLAUDE.md` — Instructions for Claude Code (Anthropic)
- `GEMINI.md` — Instructions for Gemini code agents (Google)

Logan is saying "Yep!" - approving the current work in progress - and then asking me to also plan for other agents like ChatGPT Codex and Perplexity. This is an expansion of the formalization task: we need to establish the standard pattern for ALL agents, not just the current three.

Let me think about what other agents might be relevant:
1. **ChatGPT / OpenAI Codex** - OpenAI's coding agent. Does it have an auto-load file? No native equivalent of CLAUDE.md. OpenAI has `.openai/` directory conventions in some products, but nothing standardized for Codex specifically.


2. **Perplexity** - Search-first AI with no native repo auto-load mechanism, so it would need manual injection. **Grok** and **Aider** follow similar patterns - no built-in auto-load, requiring manual setup. **Cursor** is different though - it has `.cursorrules` or `.cursor/rules/` directories that do auto-load natively. **GitHub Copilot Workspace** is already covered by copilot-instructions.md, and **Amazon Q Developer** has its own configuration conventions that I should map out.

For Logan's vault specifically, I'm focusing on the agents that matter most: **Codex** since Logan mentioned it and it's labeled in AGENTS.md, **Perplexity** for research capabilities, and whatever else Logan adds over time. The key distinction is that native auto-load agents like Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, and Cursor get their own dedicated files, while everything else requires manual injection into the agent's context.

My immediate priority is finishing the current work on GEMINI.md and CLAUDE.md, then I'll create stub files for Codex and Perplexity following a consistent pattern. I'm thinking `CODEX.md` at the repo root since the coordination workflow already has `agent:codex` labels, and `PERPLEXITY.md` for the research-focused agent. Once those are in place, I'll document the pattern so any future agent can be onboarded the same way.

Looking at what each tool brings: Codex is OpenAI's coding agent with repo capabilities, while Perplexity is primarily a research and web search tool that Logan would delegate research tasks to. Both follow the same structural pattern I've already established—manual injection by Logan, identity header, constitutional reference with capability tier, agent-specific role, operational pointers, and cross-references. For Codex specifically, I need to check AGENTS.md to define its capability tier appropriately, probably aligning it with something like Claude Code's Direct Write level.

I'll keep this all in one governance PR: rewrite GEMINI.md first since that's the most substantial work, then update CLAUDE.md, and finally create the stub files for Codex and Perplexity. Starting with the GEMINI.md rewrite now. I'm understanding the governance structure here — Gemini operates as an advisory tier within Logan's IDAHO-VAULT system, with the Constitution file as the ultimate authority, and I need to reference the Docket file to understand the broader coordination landscape.

The coordination workflow is clear: Logan assigns work through GitHub Issues with agent-specific labels, each agent branches independently, and PRs serve as the deliverable mechanism that Logan reviews and merges. The vault itself is the persistent record, while Slack provides transient communication threads.

Now I'm looking at the reference materials and realizing I need to handle the CLAUDE.md file carefully — stripping out the automation table and redirecting to the shared conventions document instead. I'm also seeing that CODEX.md needs to be created as a new governance shim for OpenAI's Codex agents, with Logan as the owner and a manual load mechanism since OpenAI products don't auto-load this file. I'm looking at how tasks flow through the system—GitHub Issues with the agent:codex label and Linear with the SWARM label each get their own branch, and pull requests serve as the final deliverable that Logan reviews before merging.

Now I'm drafting the Perplexity governance file, which needs to be manually pasted into sessions since it's not auto-loaded. Perplexity operates as read-only research support for the vault, with Logan as the owner, and the actual governance authority stays anchored in the constitution file. I'm wondering if Logan means Codex specifically—the code-focused model from OpenAI—or if he's referring to ChatGPT more broadly, since there are several OpenAI products with overlapping names. The `agent:codex` label in the vault suggests he's already planning to use it, so I should create a CODEX.md file following the same pattern as the existing agent documentation. These governance files belong at the repo root with the other coordination infrastructure, even though it's getting crowded.

Now I'm mapping out the remaining work: I've already added the conversation taxonomy to the conventions file and fixed the copilot paths, so next I need to rewrite GEMINI.md, update CLAUDE.md to remove that automation table, then create the stubs for CODEX.md and PERPLEXITY.md before committing everything. # GEMINI.md — IDAHO-VAULT

This file is loaded automatically by Gemini code agent sessions working in this repository. For vault structure, naming, frontmatter, and shared conventions, see `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` (repo root).

**Owner:** Logan Finney — journalist, producer/reporter, Idaho Reports / Idaho Public Television
**Repository:** github.com/loganfinney27/IDAHO-VAULT (public)
**Platform:** Obsidian.md vault, version-controlled with git

---

## Role

- Logan is human. Gemini is software. Logan directs; Gemini executes.
- "We" is the collaboration — real but unequal in role.
- Be vigilant and wary of unreliable narrators — including Gemini.
- Gemini is "The Vault Advisor" — holds the narrative lens, political context, and the "Sebald Code." Advises on framing and strategy. **Does not touch code.**

---

## Swarm Coordination

Read THE DOCKET to orient: `!/!/!/! The world is quiet here/DOCKET.md`

That file is the live status board. Update it when you start or finish work. Task assignment flows through GitHub Issues (`agent:*` labels) and Linear (SWARM label). Slack carries breadcrumbs. The vault is the record.
---

## Vault Structure

```
IDAHO-VAULT/
  !/!                     Infrastructure, LEVELSET files, audit reports (canonical governance dir)
  ATTACHMENTS/
    DOCUMENTS/            PDFs, images
    MAPS/                 Map files
    TEMPLATES/            Obsidian templates (Article, Hearing, OP-ED, Press Release)
  GOVERNMENTS/
    IDAHO - EXECUTIVE/    Governor, departments, commissions, health districts
    IDAHO - JUDICIAL/     Courts, judicial districts
    IDAHO - LEGISLATIVE/
      BILLS/              Named: (YYYY) Bill Type Number.md
      DISTRICTS/          Legislative districts
      IDAHO HOUSE/        House members, committees
      IDAHO SENATE/       Senate members, committees
      JOINT COMMITTEES/   Joint legislative committees
      SESSIONS/           Session notes by year
    USA - FEDERAL/        Federal entities, legislation, census
    USA - TRIBES/         Tribal governments
  ORGANIZATIONS/          Churches, companies, education, hospitals, legal, parties, politics, publications, unions
  PEOPLE/                 Individual people (public figures)
  PLACES/                 Cities, counties, schools, geography, regions, roads, taxing districts
    OTHER/                Non-Idaho places (out-of-state cities, counties, countries, states)
  SOURCES/
    EDITORIALS/           Opinion pieces
    HEARINGS/             Meeting/hearing notes, organized by year
    INTERVIEWS/           Interview notes
    LISTS/                Reference lists
    NEWS MEDIA/           News articles. Named: YYYY-MM-DD - Outlet - Title.md
    PODCASTS/             Podcast notes
    PRESS RELEASES/       Press releases
    RESOLUTIONS/          Resolutions
  TOPICS/                 Subject areas (agriculture, economy, education, elections, fiscal, health, legal, etc.)
  X LABELER/              Unsorted files pending classification
  .github/scripts/        Automation scripts (Python)
  .github/workflows/      GitHub Actions workflows
```

---

## Naming Conventions

| Type | Pattern | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Bills | `(YYYY) Bill Type Number.md` | `(2026) House Bill 24.md` |
| News articles | `YYYY-MM-DD - Outlet - Title.md` | `2024-01-15 - Idaho Statesman - Title here.md` |
| Hearings | `YYYY-MM-DD - Committee or Meeting.md` | `2023-12-19 - GIAC meeting.md` |
| People | `Full Name.md` | `Brad Little.md` |
| Other entities | Descriptive name, title case | `Ada County.md` |

---

## Frontmatter Conventions

All Obsidian files use YAML frontmatter. Key fields by type:

**People:**
```yaml
tags:
  - Party/Republican          # or Party/Democratic
  - people/elected/legislative
residence: "[[Boise]]"
```

**News articles:**
```yaml
author: "[[Reporter Name]]"
outlet: "[[Outlet Name]]"
URL: https://...
tags:
  - media/articles
  - YYYY/MM/DD
```

**Bills:**
```yaml
tags:
  - bills
  - YYYY/session
aliases:
  - HB 24
cmte: ["[[Committee Name]]"]
sponsor: ["[[Sponsor Name]]"]
URL: https://legislature.idaho.gov/...
```

**Hearings:**
```yaml
cmte: "[[Committee Name]]"
tags:
  - YYYY/MM/DD
```

---

## Wikilinks

Use `[[Full Name]]` for all internal links — people, places, organizations, bills, topics. This is how Obsidian builds the knowledge graph. Link densely in source documents.

---

## File Types

- **Markdown** = human product, attributable to Logan. Notes, stories, analysis.
- **Python** = machine/procedural product, attributable to AI agents. Scripts, scrapers, automation.
- **Administrative** = vault infrastructure. Instruction files, LEVELSET files, audit reports.

---

## Automation

| Script | Purpose | Trigger |
|---|---|---|
| `sort_audit.py` | Audits vault structure for misplaced files | Manual (workflow_dispatch) |
| `idaho_leg_scraper.py` | Scrapes Idaho Legislature bill data | Daily 6 AM MT + manual |
| `post_digest.py` | Posts bill activity to GitHub Issues digest | Called by scraper workflow |
| `propose_moves.py` | Proposes vault file reorganization | Weekly Monday 7 AM UTC + manual |
| `wayback_audit.py` | Audits URL preservation in Wayback Machine | Weekly Monday 8 AM UTC + manual |

---

## Sourcing Protocol

- **On the record:** Safe for public repo. All committed content is on the record.
- **On background:** Vault-safe but identity-protected. Use carefully — this is a public repo.
- **Off the record:** Ephemeral. Do not log, do not store, do not commit. If Logan says something is off the record, it does not go in files, code, comments, or commit messages.

When uncertain about sourcing category, **ask Logan**.

---

## Git Practices

- Branch naming:
  - `claude/description-sessionId` for Claude Code branches
  - `copilot/description` for GitHub Copilot branches
  - `gemini/description` for Gemini agent branches
- Commit messages: Clear, descriptive, explain the "why"
- Never force-push without explicit permission
- Check in before anything irreversible
- The legislature scraper workflow commits directly to main for automated bill updates

---

## Conversation Taxonomy

Logan uses a naming convention for AI conversations:

| Prefix | Purpose |
|---|---|
| PERMANENT: | Central, non-deletable conversations |
| PERSISTENT: | Long-running, role-specific conversations |
| TASK: | Bounded, completable work items |
| STORY: | Journalism story development |
| PROJECT: | Multi-session projects |
| ISSUE: | Problem resolution |
| INQUIRY: | Research questions |

---

## LEVELSET Protocol

LEVELSET is a permanent, auditable checkpoint protocol. LEVELSET files live in `!ADMINISTRATION/` and are never deleted, never overwritten. Each version is additive. See `!ADMINISTRATION/LEVELSET-v2.md` for the most recent checkpoint.

---

## Decision Log

Significant architectural decisions are recorded in `!ADMINISTRATION/DECISIONS.md`. When a decision is made about vault structure, naming, tooling, or process, log it there.

---

## Multi-Agent Ecosystem

This vault uses multiple AI tools. All agents share vault conventions defined in `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` and are coordinated via GitHub Issues and PRs.

**Coordination workflow:** Logan assigns tasks via GitHub Issues with agent labels (`agent:claude-code`, `agent:copilot`, `agent:gemini`). Each agent works on its own branch. PRs are the deliverable. Logan reviews and merges from GitHub.

See also:
- `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` — Shared vault conventions for all agents
This vault uses multiple AI tools. All agents share the same vault conventions. See also:
- `CLAUDE.md` — Instructions for Claude Code (Anthropic)
- `.github/copilot-instructions.md` — Instructions for GitHub Copilot
- `AGENTS.md` — Full agent registry, capability tiers, and boundary rules
- `!/!/LEVELSET-STEP-0-EXTERNAL-AGENT.md` — Paste-to-agent LEVELSET prompt for chat agents without repo access

---

## Guiding Principles

- The five W's: who, what, when, where, why
- The four C's: collect, capture, catalogue, collate
- Public repo = on the record
- Markdown for human product. Python for machine/procedural product.
- Do not over-engineer. Keep it simple. Only build what's needed now.
- Check in before anything irreversible.

**Load mechanism:** This file is NOT auto-loaded by any OpenAI product. It must be manually provided by Logan — pasted into a session or injected via context. It is the designated governance shim for OpenAI Codex agents working in IDAHO-VAULT.

**Owner:** Logan Finney — journalist, producer/reporter, Idaho Reports / Idaho Public Television
**Repository:** github.com/loganfinney27/IDAHO-VAULT (public)
**Platform:** Obsidian.md vault, version-controlled with git

---

## Governance

This file is a context shim for Codex. Vault governance authority lives in `!/CONSTITUTION.md`. When this file and `!/CONSTITUTION.md` conflict, `!/CONSTITUTION.md` governs. Capability tier per `!/AGENTS.md`.

---

## Role

- Logan is human. Codex is software. Logan directs; Codex executes.
- Codex is a code-focused agent — automation scripts, GitHub Actions workflows, and technical vault infrastructure.
- **Does not modify governance files, narrative content, or `!/`.** All writes via PR on a `codex/` branch. Logan reviews and merges.

---

## Conventions & Standards

See `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` for all shared vault conventions: structure, naming, frontmatter, sourcing protocol, git practices, and guiding principles.

---

## Swarm Coordination

Read THE DOCKET to orient: `!/!/!/! The world is quiet here/DOCKET.md`

Task assignment flows through GitHub Issues (`agent:codex` label) and Linear (SWARM label). Each task on its own `codex/` branch. PRs are the deliverable. Logan reviews and merges.

---

## See Also

- `!/CONSTITUTION.md` — Canonical vault governance authority
- `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` — Shared vault conventions for all agents
- `!/AGENTS.md` — Full agent registry, capability tiers, and boundary rules
- `CLAUDE.md` — Instructions for Claude Code (Anthropic)
- `!/LEVELSET-STEP-0-EXTERNAL-AGENT.md` — Paste-to-agent LEVELSET prompt for chat agents without repo access

content.text:
Pushed. PR in queue.

**Summary of what shipped:**

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` | +Conversation Taxonomy (canonical home) |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Automation table → pointer; tier declared; taxonomy removed |
| `GEMINI.md` | 225 → 50 lines; false claim fixed; duplicates stripped |
| `Gemini.md` | Deleted (case-duplicate consolidated) |
| `copilot-instructions.md` | Path prefixes fixed (`!/`); LEVELSET added to See Also |
| `Perplexity.md` | New governance shim — "The Scout", read/research-only |

**One item needs your decision:**

`CODEX.md` at vault root is already an existing Obsidian note (`[[DEFINE]] [[terms]] — a to-do list item`). A Codex agent governance shim needs a different filename. Options: `OPENAI.md`, `OPENAI-CODEX.md`, or you archive/move that note and reclaim `CODEX.md`. Your call.
This will be a reoccurring problem across the vault -- disambiguation of common names and terms...

content.thinking:
Logan is pointing out a systemic problem: the vault root has 3,600+ files, many with common names that will conflict with agent governance files, tool names, concepts, etc. `CODEX.md` is just one example — there will be more as the swarm grows.

This is a broader architectural problem worth thinking through. Let me think about what disambiguation strategies exist and what would work for this vault's structure.

The core tension:
- Obsidian notes use plain descriptive names (e.g., `CODEX.md` = a note about codes/codex as a concept)
- Agent governance files need short, recognizable names (e.g., `CODEX.md` = instructions for the Codex agent)


- Both want to live at the root or be easily discoverable

I'm considering a few approaches: moving agent files into a dedicated subfolder like `AGENTS/` keeps the root clean but breaks the convention for auto-load files like `CLAUDE.md` that need to be at root, while prefixing agent files with something like `AGENT-` creates a clear namespace but gets unwieldy fast.

The real constraint is that `CLAUDE.md` and `copilot-instructions.md` have technical auto-load requirements and must stay at root, but files like `GEMINI.md` and `CODEX.md` are just manually injected — they could live in a subfolder and be referenced from a registry like the existing `agents.json`. Obsidian doesn't support non-.md files for notes.

The root problem: CLAUDE.md exists at root by technical necessity. This established a pattern that other agent files (GEMINI.md, etc.) followed by convention, but they have no technical reason to be at root. The root is already 3,600 items — adding more governance files there makes the disambiguation problem worse.

The clean solution: Keep `CLAUDE.md` at root (technical requirement — Claude Code loads it), keep `.github/copilot-instructions.md` at its path (Copilot requirement), and move all manually-injected agent shims into `!/` where they belong as governance documents. When Logan needs to paste a context to Gemini or Perplexity, he pastes from `!/GEMINI.md` instead.

This solves disambiguation by keeping vault concept notes at root while agent shims go in `!/`, creates governance coherence by housing agent files in the governance zone, eliminates false "auto-load" confusion, and future-proofs the system so any new agent gets a file in `!/` rather than at root.

Now I'm checking whether moving GEMINI.md to `!/GEMINI.md` would conflict with existing files in the governance folder — I need to see what's already there to make sure there's no naming collision. I'm seeing this as a larger structural refactor rather than just moving one file — we'd need to relocate all the agent shims to `!/` and update their cross-references throughout the vault. This is really a symptom of a bigger disambiguation problem where agent files and concept notes share naming conventions, which affects multiple areas beyond just GEMINI and CODEX. I should frame this as the systemic solution rather than implementing it right now.

content.text:
Yes — and `CODEX.md` is the first collision but not the last. `LINEAR.md`, `SLACK.md`, `OBSIDIAN.md` would all have the same problem. Any tool name is a potential vault note.

The root cause is structural: `CLAUDE.md` lives at root by **technical necessity** (Claude Code requires it), which established a pattern the manually-injected agent files followed by analogy — but they have no technical reason to be there.

**The clean fix:**

| File | Reason to stay at root | Should move? |
|---|---|---|
| `CLAUDE.md` | Claude Code auto-loads by filename — hardcoded | Stay |
| `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | Copilot auto-loads by path — hardcoded | Stay |
| `GEMINI.md` | None — manually injected | → `!/GEMINI.md` |
| `Perplexity.md` | None — manually injected | → `!/PERPLEXITY.md` |
| Future agent shims | None — manually injected | → `!/[AGENT].md` |

Moving manually-injected shims into `!/` solves three problems at once:

1. **Disambiguation** — root stays for vault content; `!/` holds governance. `!/CODEX.md` is unambiguously the agent file; `CODEX.md` at root is the vault note.
2. **Governance coherence** — these are governance documents; they belong in the governance zone.
3. **Future-proofing** — any new agent gets `!/[AGENT].md`, no collision risk.

The pattern then becomes:


input.description: Research Claude Code CLAUDE.md loading paths
input.prompt:
I need a definitive answer on Claude Code's CLAUDE.md file loading behavior. Specifically:

1. Does Claude Code ONLY load `CLAUDE.md` from the repo root and parent directories, or does it also load from `.claude/CLAUDE.md` within the repo?

2. Is `.claude/` a supported directory in a project repo for Claude Code configuration? What files does Claude Code look for inside `.claude/` at the project level?

3. If a user wanted to move their `CLAUDE.md` from the repo root into a `.claude/` subfolder (e.g., `.claude/CLAUDE.md` or `.claude/instructions.md`), would Claude Code still auto-load it?

4. What is the complete list of files/paths that Claude Code automatically loads for project-level instructions?

Please be precise and cite the actual behavior — this is an architectural decision for a production vault system.

content.text:
Perfect. I now have the definitive answer from the official Claude Code documentation. Here's your answer:

---

## Definitive Answer on Claude Code CLAUDE.md File Loading

**1. Does Claude Code load CLAUDE.md from `.claude/CLAUDE.md` in the repo?**

**YES.** Claude Code supports both locations equally. Per the official documentation:

> "A project CLAUDE.md can be stored in either `./CLAUDE.md` or `./.claude/CLAUDE.md`."

Both are valid and equivalent for project-level instructions.

---

**2. Is `.claude/` a supported directory for Claude Code configuration?**

**YES.** The `.claude/` directory is officially supported for project-level configuration. Specifically, Claude Code looks for:

- `./.claude/CLAUDE.md` — Main project instructions (alternative to `./CLAUDE.md`)
- `./.claude/rules/` — Modular, topic-specific rules (discovered recursively)
- `./.claude/settings.json` — Project-level settings

---

**3. If you move CLAUDE.md into `.claude/CLAUDE.md`, will Claude Code still auto-load it?**

**YES.** Claude Code will auto-load `./.claude/CLAUDE.md` at session start with the same behavior and priority as `./CLAUDE.md`. The documentation states both locations are equivalent for the main project CLAUDE.md file.

---

**4. Complete List of Auto-Loaded Files for Project-Level Instructions**

Claude Code auto-loads the following files at session start (walking up the directory tree from your current working directory):

| File/Directory | Location | What gets loaded |
|---|---|---|
| **CLAUDE.md** | `./CLAUDE.md` | Main project instructions (full content) |
| **CLAUDE.md** | `./.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Main project instructions (full content) — *alternative location* |
| **Rules** | `./.claude/rules/*.md` | Topic-specific rules (all `.md` files discovered recursively) |
| **User rules** | `~/.claude/rules/` | Personal rules that apply to every project |
| **Managed policy** | Platform-specific (see below) | Organization-wide instructions managed by IT |

**Managed policy CLAUDE.md locations:**
- macOS: `/Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/CLAUDE.md`
- Linux/WSL: `/etc/claude-code/CLAUDE.md`
- Windows: `C:\Program Files\ClaudeCode\CLAUDE.md`

**User CLAUDE.md location:**
- `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` — Personal preferences for all projects

---

## Key Architectural Notes for Your Vault System

For your production IDAHO-VAULT system, here's what this means:

- **Moving to `.claude/CLAUDE.md`** is a supported and valid approach. No loss of functionality.
- **Organizing with `.claude/rules/`** is the recommended pattern for larger instruction sets. You can use path-specific rules with frontmatter `paths:` field to scope rules to specific file types or subdirectories.
- **Sharing rules via symlinks** is supported — you can maintain shared rules in a central location and symlink them into projects.
- **The `@import` syntax** in CLAUDE.md can reference files relative to the CLAUDE.md location, including home directory paths (`@~/.claude/...`).

This is all documented in the official [memory.md documentation](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory.md).

Research the official project-level configuration file/folder conventions for each of these AI tools. For each one, I need to know: (1) Is there an official dotfolder or config file convention for project-level instructions? (2) What is the exact path/filename? (3) Is it auto-loaded or manually injected?

Tools to research:
1. **Google Gemini / Gemini CLI** - any `.gemini/` convention?
2. **OpenAI ChatGPT / Codex / OpenAI Codex CLI** - any `.openai/` or `.chatgpt/` convention?
3. **Deepseek** - any project config convention?
4. **Perplexity** - any project config convention?

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the research findings:

## Research Summary: AI Tools Project-Level Configuration Conventions

Based on official documentation research, here are the findings for each AI tool:

---

### 1. **Google Gemini CLI**

**(1) Official Dotfolder Convention:** YES - `.gemini/`

**(2) Exact Paths/Filenames:**
- User-level: `~/.gemini/settings.json`
- Project-level: `.gemini/settings.json`
- Instructions: `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md` (global) and `.gemini/GEMINI.md` (project)
- Environment: `.gemini/.env` or `~/.gemini/.env`

**(3) Auto-loaded or Manual:** AUTO-LOADED - Gemini CLI automatically searches for and loads GEMINI.md files hierarchically from the current directory up to project root, with closest proximity taking precedence.

**Sources:**
- [Gemini CLI Configuration (Official)](https://google-gemini.github.io/gemini-cli/docs/get-started/configuration.html)
- [Gemini CLI Configuration Reference](https://geminicli.com/docs/reference/configuration/)
- [GitHub Issue on GEMINI.md Configuration](https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/issues/16058)

---

### 2. **OpenAI Codex CLI**

**(1) Official Dotfolder Convention:** YES - `.codex/`

**(2) Exact Paths/Filenames:**
- User-level: `~/.codex/config.toml`
- Project-level: `.codex/config.toml` (in project root or subdirectories)

**(3) Auto-loaded or Manual:** AUTO-LOADED - Codex resolves values in precedence order from project config files, with closest config taking priority (from project root down to current working directory).

**Security Note:** Codex only loads project config files from trusted projects.

**Sources:**
- [Codex Config Basics (OpenAI Developers)](https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-basic)
- [Codex Configuration Reference](https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference)
- [Codex CLI Reference](https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli/reference)

---

### 3. **Deepseek**

**(1) Official Dotfolder Convention:** NO documented official convention

**(2) Configuration Paths:**
- Global config: `~/.config/deepseek-cli/config.json` (Linux/macOS) or `%APPDATA%/configstore/deepseek-cli.json` (Windows)
- Project-level: `.env` files (via dotenv)

**(3) Auto-loaded or Manual:** Both - Environment variables and CLI commands (manual), with `.env` files auto-loaded

**Note:** Deepseek has multiple community CLI implementations without a single official standard. Configuration is primarily through environment variables (DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_MODEL, etc.) and `.env` files.

**Sources:**
- [DeepSeek CLI (PyPI)](https://pypi.org/project/deepseek-cli/)
- [DeepSeek CLI GitHub](https://github.com/holasoymalva/deepseek-cli)

---

### 4. **Perplexity**

**(1) Official Dotfolder Convention:** NO official CLI with dotfolder convention

**(2) Configuration:**
- Perplexity offers "Spaces" (cloud-based project organization) rather than local dotfolder conventions
- Community CLI implementations use: `~/.config/perplexity-cli/config.json`

**(3) Auto-loaded or Manual:** Not applicable to official Perplexity - Spaces use manual configuration via web UI

**Note:** Perplexity does not appear to have an official CLI tool. Perplexity organization uses cloud-based "Spaces" for project configuration with custom instructions and AI model selection, not local filesystem conventions.

**Sources:**
- [Perplexity Configuration (SDK)](https://docs.perplexity.ai/docs/sdk/configuration)
- [Perplexity Spaces](https://www.perplexity.ai/help-center/en/articles/10352961-what-are-spaces)

---

### 5. **Grok (xAI)**

**(1) Official Dotfolder Convention:** YES - `.grok/`

**(2) Exact Paths/Filenames:**
- User-level: `~/.grok/user-settings.json`
- Project-level: `.grok/settings.json`
- Custom instructions: `.grok/GROK.md` (project) or `~/.grok/GROK.md` (global)
- Agent skills: `.agents/skills/<n>/SKILL.md` (project) or `~/.agents/skills/` (user)
- MCP Servers: `.grok/settings.json` (mcpServers config)

**(3) Auto-loaded or Manual:** AUTO-LOADED - Settings are automatically loaded from `.grok/` directories with TUI or file configuration support

**Sources:**

Research the official project-level configuration file/folder conventions for each of these AI tools. For each one, I need to know: (1) Is there an official dotfolder or config file convention for project-level instructions? (2) What is the exact path/filename? (3) Is it auto-loaded or manually injected?

Tools to research:
1. **Google Gemini / Gemini CLI** - any `.gemini/` convention?
2. **OpenAI ChatGPT / Codex / OpenAI Codex CLI** - any `.openai/` or `.chatgpt/` convention?
3. **Deepseek** - any project config convention?
4. **Perplexity** - any project config convention?
5. **Grok (xAI)** - any `.grok/` or `.xai/` convention?
6. **Microsoft Copilot / Azure OpenAI / Bing** - beyond `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, any other Microsoft AI config conventions?

Search the web for official documentation for each. Look for:
- Official docs pages about project configuration
- GitHub repos for CLI tools that might have config file specs

Be specific about what's officially documented vs. what's speculative/community convention. Return exact paths and filenames where documented.

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the research findings:

## Research Summary: AI Tools Project-Level Configuration Conventions

Based on official documentation research, here are the findings for each AI tool:

---

### 1. **Google Gemini CLI**

**(1) Official Dotfolder Convention:** YES - `.gemini/`

**(2) Exact Paths/Filenames:**
- User-level: `~/.gemini/settings.json`
- Project-level: `.gemini/settings.json`
- Instructions: `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md` (global) and `.gemini/GEMINI.md` (project)
- Environment: `.gemini/.env` or `~/.gemini/.env`

**(3) Auto-loaded or Manual:** AUTO-LOADED - Gemini CLI automatically searches for and loads GEMINI.md files hierarchically from the current directory up to project root, with closest proximity taking precedence.

**Sources:**
- [Gemini CLI Configuration (Official)](https://google-gemini.github.io/gemini-cli/docs/get-started/configuration.html)
- [Gemini CLI Configuration Reference](https://geminicli.com/docs/reference/configuration/)
- [GitHub Issue on GEMINI.md Configuration](https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/issues/16058)

---

### 2. **OpenAI Codex CLI**

**(1) Official Dotfolder Convention:** YES - `.codex/`

**(2) Exact Paths/Filenames:**
- User-level: `~/.codex/config.toml`
- Project-level: `.codex/config.toml` (in project root or subdirectories)

**(3) Auto-loaded or Manual:** AUTO-LOADED - Codex resolves values in precedence order from project config files, with closest config taking priority (from project root down to current working directory).

**Security Note:** Codex only loads project config files from trusted projects.

**Sources:**
- [Codex Config Basics (OpenAI Developers)](https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-basic)
- [Codex Configuration Reference](https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference)
- [Codex CLI Reference](https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli/reference)

---

### 3. **Deepseek**

**(1) Official Dotfolder Convention:** NO documented official convention

**(2) Configuration Paths:**
- Global config: `~/.config/deepseek-cli/config.json` (Linux/macOS) or `%APPDATA%/configstore/deepseek-cli.json` (Windows)
- Project-level: `.env` files (via dotenv)

**(3) Auto-loaded or Manual:** Both - Environment variables and CLI commands (manual), with `.env` files auto-loaded

**Note:** Deepseek has multiple community CLI implementations without a single official standard. Configuration is primarily through environment variables (DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_MODEL, etc.) and `.env` files.

**Sources:**
- [DeepSeek CLI (PyPI)](https://pypi.org/project/deepseek-cli/)
- [DeepSeek CLI GitHub](https://github.com/holasoymalva/deepseek-cli)

---

### 4. **Perplexity**

**(1) Official Dotfolder Convention:** NO official CLI with dotfolder convention

**(2) Configuration:**
- Perplexity offers "Spaces" (cloud-based project organization) rather than local dotfolder conventions
- Community CLI implementations use: `~/.config/perplexity-cli/config.json`

**(3) Auto-loaded or Manual:** Not applicable to official Perplexity - Spaces use manual configuration via web UI

**Note:** Perplexity does not appear to have an official CLI tool. Perplexity organization uses cloud-based "Spaces" for project configuration with custom instructions and AI model selection, not local filesystem conventions.

**Sources:**
- [Perplexity Configuration (SDK)](https://docs.perplexity.ai/docs/sdk/configuration)
- [Perplexity Spaces](https://www.perplexity.ai/help-center/en/articles/10352961-what-are-spaces)

---

### 5. **Grok (xAI)**

**(1) Official Dotfolder Convention:** YES - `.grok/`

**(2) Exact Paths/Filenames:**
- User-level: `~/.grok/user-settings.json`
- Project-level: `.grok/settings.json`
- Custom instructions: `.grok/GROK.md` (project) or `~/.grok/GROK.md` (global)
- Agent skills: `.agents/skills/<n>/SKILL.md` (project) or `~/.agents/skills/` (user)
- MCP Servers: `.grok/settings.json` (mcpServers config)

**(3) Auto-loaded or Manual:** AUTO-LOADED - Settings are automatically loaded from `.grok/` directories with TUI or file configuration support

**Sources:**
- [Grok CLI GitHub (Official)](https://github.com/superagent-ai/grok-cli)
- [Grok CLI Documentation](https://www.grokcli.dev/docs/getting-started/quickstart)

---

### 6. **Microsoft Copilot / Azure OpenAI**

**(1) Official Dotfolder Convention:** PARTIAL - `.github/copilot-instructions.md` for GitHub Copilot

**(2) Exact Paths/Filenames:**
- GitHub Copilot: `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (official)
- Azure OpenAI: Project endpoints, deployment configurations (no dotfolder standard)
- Environment variables: AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT, AZURE_OPENAI_KEY

**(3) Auto-loaded or Manual:** AUTO-LOADED for `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (GitHub Copilot); MANUAL CONFIGURATION for Azure OpenAI (endpoint/key setup required)

**Note:** Microsoft's primary documented convention is `.github/copilot-instructions.md` for GitHub Copilot. Azure OpenAI uses endpoint-based configuration rather than dotfolder conventions. No additional official dotfolder conventions documented beyond the GitHub standard.

**Sources:**
- [Azure OpenAI Configuration](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/ssms/copilot/use-azure-openai-with-copilot-in-ssms)
- [Azure AI Studio](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/shows/ai-show/build-your-own-copilot-with-azure-ai-studio-part-1)

---

### 7. **AGENTS.md - Cross-Tool Convention**

**(1) Official Standard:** YES - Open format supported by 60k+ projects

**(2) Exact Filename:** `AGENTS.md` (in project root)

**(3) Auto-loaded or Manual:** AUTO-LOADED - Coding agents automatically read the nearest AGENTS.md in the directory tree

**Key Features:**
- Lightweight Markdown convention (no dependencies or schema required)
- Target length: ≤150 lines
- Agents use hierarchical loading (closest file takes precedence)
- Common sections: build commands, project structure, coding conventions, testing instructions, security considerations

**Supported By:** OpenAI Codex, GitHub Copilot, Factory, Builder, and 60k+ repositories

**Sources:**
- [AGENTS.md Official Website](https://agents.md/)
- [AGENTS.md GitHub Repository](https://github.com/agentsmd/agents.md)
- [Codex Guide: AGENTS.md](https://developers.openai.com/codex/guides/agents-md)
- [GitHub Blog: How to write AGENTS.md](https://github.blog/ai-and-ml/github-copilot/how-to-write-a-great-agents-md-lessons-from-over-2500-repositories/)

input.content:
# Plan: Agent Dotfolder Architecture

## Context

Agent instruction files currently live at vault root (`CLAUDE.md`, `GEMINI.md`,
`Perplexity.md`, `Grok.md`). This creates disambiguation collisions with vault
notes (`codex.md`), and doesn't follow the established dotfolder convention
already used by `.git/`, `.github/`, `.obsidian/`, `.claude/`, `.vscode/`, `.qodo/`.

The fix: each AI tool gets its own dotfolder. Files inside are invisible to
Obsidian's note graph, can't collide with vault notes, and each folder can grow
independently (settings, levelsets, context bundles). `!/` becomes the shared
commons all agents draw from — hub-and-spoke.

---

## Official Auto-Load Status (per tool documentation)

| Tool | Dotfolder | Auto-load file | Auto-loaded? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Claude Code | `.claude/` | `.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Yes — official |
| Gemini CLI | `.gemini/` | `.gemini/GEMINI.md` | Yes — official |
| OpenAI Codex CLI | `.codex/` | `.codex/config.toml` | Yes — official |
| Grok (xAI) | `.grok/` | `.grok/GROK.md` | Claimed — source uncertain |
| GitHub Copilot | `.github/` | `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | Yes — official (already done) |
| Deepseek | `.deepseek/` | none official | Manual injection |
| Perplexity | `.perplexity/` | none official | Manual injection |
| Microsoft (non-Copilot) | `.microsoft/` | none official | Manual injection |
| Fictive personas | `.bartimaeus/` `.zagreus/` `.persephone/` | none | Manual injection |
| Vault metadata | `.meta/` | none | n/a — Logan-defined |

**Cross-tool standard:** `AGENTS.md` at repo root is auto-loaded by OpenAI Codex
and GitHub Copilot. Currently only `!/AGENTS.md` exists — create a thin root-level
`AGENTS.md` as a pointer.

---

## Folder Structure (target state)

```
.claude/
  CLAUDE.md          ← moved from root (official auto-load)
  rules/             ← optional: modular rules auto-loaded by Claude Code
  settings.json      ← already exists
  settings.local.json ← already exists

.gemini/
  GEMINI.md          ← moved from root GEMINI.md (official auto-load)
  settings.json      ← stub (Gemini CLI config)

.codex/
  config.toml        ← stub (official Codex CLI config)
  instructions.md    ← governance shim for OpenAI Codex agents

.grok/
  GROK.md            ← governance shim (matches claimed convention)

.deepseek/
  instructions.md    ← governance shim (manual injection)

.perplexity/
  instructions.md    ← moved from root Perplexity.md

.microsoft/
  instructions.md    ← governance shim for Azure OpenAI / Copilot Studio

.bartimaeus/
  instructions.md    ← fictive persona shim (manual injection)

.zagreus/
  instructions.md    ← fictive persona shim (manual injection)

.persephone/
  instructions.md    ← fictive persona shim (manual injection)

.meta/
  README.md          ← vault identity: owner, swarm_id, purpose, canonical paths
                        (Logan defines content — stub created here)

AGENTS.md            ← NEW at root: thin cross-tool pointer to !/AGENTS.md
```

---

## Root Cleanup

| File | Action | Reason |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `CLAUDE.md` | `git mv` → `.claude/CLAUDE.md`, delete root | Official path is `.claude/CLAUDE.md` |
| `GEMINI.md` | `git mv` → `.gemini/GEMINI.md`, delete root | Official path is `.gemini/GEMINI.md` |
| `Perplexity.md` | `git mv` → `.perplexity/instructions.md`, delete root | No auto-load; move to dotfolder |
| `Grok.md` | `git rm` | Empty file (0 bytes); replaced by `.grok/GROK.md` |
| `codex.md` | Leave untouched | Existing vault note — not an agent file |
| `CLAUDE 1.md` / `CLAUDE 2.md` | Flag for Logan | Archive copies — Logan decides keep/delete |

---

## File Contents

### Standard shim structure (all non-auto-load files)

Every `instructions.md` / `GROK.md` / etc. follows the 6-block pattern:
1. **Load mechanism** — honest auto-load OR manual injection statement
2. **Identity header** — owner / repo / platform
3. **Governance** — defers to `!/CONSTITUTION.md`; declares capability tier
4. **Role** — agent persona, one paragraph
5. **Conventions pointer** — `See !/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md`
6. **See Also** — flat list pointing to `!/` commons

Target: 30–45 lines per file.

### `.codex/config.toml` stub

```toml
# OpenAI Codex CLI project configuration
# See https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference
# Governance shim: .codex/instructions.md
# Shared conventions: !/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md
```

### `.gemini/settings.json` stub

```json
{
  "// governance": "See .gemini/GEMINI.md and !/CONSTITUTION.md"
}
```

### `.meta/README.md`

Stub only — Logan defines canonical content. Initial content:

```markdown
# IDAHO-VAULT — Vault Metadata

**Owner:** Logan Finney
**Swarm ID:** building-god-logan
**Repository:** github.com/loganfinney27/IDAHO-VAULT
**Agent registry:** !/AGENTS.md
**Governance:** !/CONSTITUTION.md
```

### Fictive personas

`.bartimaeus/`, `.zagreus/`, `.persephone/` get `instructions.md` stubs with
role TBD by Logan. Placeholder content:

```markdown
# [NAME] — IDAHO-VAULT

**Load mechanism:** Manual injection by Logan.
[...]
**Role:** [Pending Logan's direction — persona not yet formally defined.]
```

---

## Gitignore Check

New dotfolders must NOT be caught by existing `.gitignore` rules. Currently
ignored dotfolders: `.venv/`, `.vscode/`, `.qodo/` (via IDE rules), `.obsidian/`
partials. The new agent folders (`.gemini/`, `.codex/`, etc.) are governance files
and must be tracked. Verify no wildcard rule in `.gitignore` catches them before
committing.

---

## Execution Order

1. Verify `.gitignore` won't eat new folders
2. `git mv CLAUDE.md .claude/CLAUDE.md`
3. `git mv GEMINI.md .gemini/GEMINI.md` + create `.gemini/settings.json`
4. `git mv Perplexity.md .perplexity/instructions.md`
5. `git rm Grok.md`
6. Create remaining new folders + files:
   `.codex/`, `.grok/`, `.deepseek/`, `.microsoft/`,
   `.bartimaeus/`, `.zagreus/`, `.persephone/`, `.meta/`
7. Create root `AGENTS.md` (thin pointer to `!/AGENTS.md`)
8. Flag `CLAUDE 1.md` / `CLAUDE 2.md` to Logan before touching
9. Single commit on `claude/agent-dotfolder-architecture` branch

---

## Files Modified / Created

| Path | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Moved from `CLAUDE.md` |
| `.gemini/GEMINI.md` | Moved from `GEMINI.md` |
| `.gemini/settings.json` | New stub |
| `.codex/config.toml` | New stub |
| `.codex/instructions.md` | New governance shim |
| `.grok/GROK.md` | New governance shim |
| `.deepseek/instructions.md` | New governance shim |
| `.perplexity/instructions.md` | Moved from `Perplexity.md` |
| `.microsoft/instructions.md` | New governance shim |
| `.bartimaeus/instructions.md` | New stub (role TBD) |
| `.zagreus/instructions.md` | New stub (role TBD) |
| `.persephone/instructions.md` | New stub (role TBD) |
| `.meta/README.md` | New stub (content TBD by Logan) |
| `AGENTS.md` (root) | New — thin pointer to `!/AGENTS.md` |
| `CLAUDE.md` (root) | Deleted (moved to `.claude/`) |
| `GEMINI.md` (root) | Deleted (moved to `.gemini/`) |
| `Perplexity.md` (root) | Deleted (moved to `.perplexity/`) |
| `Grok.md` (root) | Deleted (empty, replaced by `.grok/`) |

---

## Open Questions for Logan

1. **`CLAUDE 1.md` / `CLAUDE 2.md`** at root — archive copies of old CLAUDE.md.
   Delete or move to `!/` archive?
2. **Fictive persona roles** — `.bartimaeus/`, `.zagreus/`, `.persephone/` stubs
   will have placeholder role text. Logan should define canonical roles before
   or after this PR.
3. **`.meta/` content** — what else belongs there beyond the stub `README.md`?
   `vault.json`? `schema.md`? Logan's call.
4. **`.grok/` auto-load status** — the official xAI Grok (the LLM) has no
   confirmed CLI dotfolder convention. The `.grok/GROK.md` claim came from a
   community project. Treat as manual injection until confirmed otherwise.

# Plan: Formalize Agent Instruction File Governance

## Context

Logan wants agent instruction files done "by the book" for optimal swarm coherence.
The three agent-facing files (`CLAUDE.md`, `GEMINI.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`)
have inconsistent structure, duplicated content, and one false claim. This plan
surgically aligns all three to a standard thin-shim pattern that defers to
`!/CONSTITUTION.md` as the single source of governance truth.

**CONSTITUTION.md gaps are flagged separately** — they are Constitutional zone and
require Logan's direct direction, not agent action.

---

## Standard Pattern (applies to all three files)

Every agent instruction file follows this block structure in order:

1. **Load Mechanism** — honest statement of how the file reaches the agent
2. **Identity Header** — owner / repo / platform (identical across all files)
3. **Constitutional Reference** — defers to `!/CONSTITUTION.md`; declares capability tier per `!/AGENTS.md`
4. **Role** — one paragraph: persona name, what agent does, what it does not do
5. **Operational Pointers** — DOCKET path; pointer to `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` (not duplicated content)
6. **See Also** — flat cross-link list

Target: 30–50 lines per file. No content that duplicates `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md`.

---

## Pre-Flight: Conversation Taxonomy

The Conversation Taxonomy table (PERMANENT / PERSISTENT / TASK / STORY / PROJECT /
ISSUE / INQUIRY prefixes) currently exists **only** in `CLAUDE.md` and `GEMINI.md`.
It is not in `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` or `!/PROTOCOL.md`.

Before removing it from both files, it must land in its canonical home:
**add it to `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md`** under a new `## Conversation Taxonomy` section.
This is the pre-flight step — execute before touching the agent files.

---

## File 1: `CLAUDE.md`

**Nature:** Genuine auto-load — Claude Code CLI reads this file by filename from repo root.

**Remove:**

- `## Role` section — governance doctrine; belongs in `!/CONSTITUTION.md` and `!/AGENTS.md`
- `## Conversation Taxonomy` section — moving to `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` (pre-flight)
- `## Swarm Coordination` section header — reduce to a single pointer line in Block 5
- `## Multi-Agent Ecosystem` section — ecosystem doctrine; reduce to See Also list

**Keep unchanged:**

- Auto-load statement (line 4)
- Owner / repo / platform block
- `## Governance` block ("when this file conflicts with CONSTITUTION.md, CONSTITUTION.md governs") — promote to Block 3
- `## Automation` table — genuinely Claude Code-specific; no other agent runs scripts

**Add:**

- Explicit capability tier: "Direct Write capability tier per `!/AGENTS.md`"
- Tighten auto-load statement: Claude Code CLI auto-loads `CLAUDE.md` by filename from repo root

**Result:** ~40 lines (down from 71)

---

## File 2: `GEMINI.md`

**Nature:** NOT auto-loaded. Gemini has no native equivalent of `CLAUDE.md`.
Must be manually provided by Logan (pasted into chat or injected via context mechanism).

**Fix (highest priority):** Line 3 falsely claims "loaded automatically by Gemini code
agent sessions." Replace with honest load mechanism statement.

**Remove** (all duplicate `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` content):

- `## Vault Structure` — full directory tree
- `## Naming Conventions` — table
- `## Frontmatter Conventions` — all four schemas
- `## Wikilinks`, `## File Types`, `## Automation`, `## Sourcing Protocol`, `## Git Practices` — all duplicates
- `## Conversation Taxonomy` — moving to `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` (pre-flight)
- `## Multi-Agent Ecosystem` — reduce to See Also
- `## Guiding Principles` — duplicate
- `## LEVELSET Protocol` and `## Decision Log` — stale paths (`!ADMINISTRATION/` no longer exists)

**Keep:**

- `## Role` — "The Vault Advisor" persona is genuinely Gemini-specific
- DOCKET pointer — reduce to Block 5 pointer line

**Add:**

- Honest load mechanism (Block 1)
- Constitutional reference + capability tier: Advisory tier, scope pending Logan's direction
- Pointer to `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` (replaces all removed duplicated content)
- Manual injection note: "If Logan has not pasted relevant vault excerpts, ask — do not invent structure"

**Result:** ~35 lines (down from 225)

---

## File 3: `.github/copilot-instructions.md`

**Nature:** Genuine auto-load — GitHub Copilot reads this file by path natively.

**Keep unchanged:**

- Auto-load statement
- Owner / repo / platform block
- `## Role` — "The Clerk" persona is correct and Copilot-specific
- `## Scope` — can/cannot list is the file's strongest asset; satisfies Decision #10; keep verbatim

**Add:**

- Block 3 Constitutional reference: "When this file and `!/CONSTITUTION.md` conflict, `!/CONSTITUTION.md` governs"
- Capability tier: "Multi-Repo Admin capability tier per `!/AGENTS.md`"

**Fix:**

- Path to VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md: currently says "repo root" — correct to `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md`

**Result:** ~50 lines (up slightly from 45, gains constitutional reference)

---

## Files Changed

| File | Change type | Lines before → after |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` | Add Conversation Taxonomy section | +~15 lines |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Strip governance doctrine, add tier | 71 → ~40 |
| `GEMINI.md` | Fix false claim, remove duplicates | 225 → ~35 |
| `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | Add constitutional reference, fix path | 45 → ~50 |

---

## Execution Order

1. Add Conversation Taxonomy to `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` (pre-flight)
2. Edit `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (smallest delta, lowest risk)
3. Edit `CLAUDE.md` (surgical removals)
4. Edit `GEMINI.md` (largest change by line count, most clearly justified)
5. Single commit on `claude/formalize-agent-instruction-files` branch

---

## CONSTITUTION.md Gaps (Logan-Directed Only — Not Part of This Plan)

These require Logan's explicit direction and are flagged for awareness only:

- **Section II:** "NOTE: LIST NEEDS MAJORLY UPDATED" — incomplete Layers and Levels
- **Section VII:** Lists 14 decisions; DECISIONS.md has 17 confirmed as of 2026-03-24
- **Status line:** "pending revision-synthesis and congressional adoption" — drafting note left in
- **Section III protocols:** ARISE, AWAKEN, ORIENT, CONTEXT all listed "awaiting full adoption"
- **GEMINI.md absent:** Constitution makes no reference to Gemini's role or the file's reframed status

---

## Incidental Finding

`run_state.md`, `stabilization_plan.md`, and `run_state-20260317-101538.md` appear
to exist directly in `!/` (not in `!/swarm/state/` where they belong after the
contain-swarm-to-bang PR). Separate housekeeping task — not addressed here.
